headline: Lorenzo enzoBTC: Bitcoin wrapped token protocol with EVM deployments; 2 key smart contracts identified on Ethereum and testnets; primary operations on Bitcoin and BNB Chain
short_headline: enzoBTC + vault contracts across EVM chains

findings.text: D1: enzoBTC token surfaced on Ethereum (0x6a9a65b84843f5fd4ac9a0471c4fc11afffbce4a) via Ethplorer name-tag search; Sepolia testnet variant at 0x1349A8d352b1971CbEbeacF55Fb75526F47B6610

D2: Canonical GitBook documentation located at https://lorenzo-protocol.gitbook.io/docs; no explicit 'Deployed Contracts' page found; site structure indicates sections for Lorenzo Governance Token (BANK), Financial Abstraction Layer, and Bitcoin Liquidity Layer

D3: GitHub audit-report repository discovered (https://github.com/Lorenzo-Protocol/audit-report); audit reports exist covering vault contracts and staking mechanisms; May 2025 audits by third-party firms identified minor reentrancy risks in FBTC_Vault and enzoBTC contracts, subsequently patched

D4: GitHub Lorenzo-Protocol organization contains 29 repositories including enzoBTC_contract, lorenzo_vault_contract, lorenzo (main), lorenzo-sdk, and supporting infrastructure; enzoBTC_contract repository uses Foundry/Solidity; last updated July 11, 2025; no deployment artifacts (broadcast or deployment JSON) directly accessible from fetched README

D5: Protocol.chains specified as 'Bitcoin' (singular); protocol operates on Bitcoin natively, BNB Chain as primary EVM deployment, with cross-chain interoperability via Wormhole and LayerZero; discovered EVM contract deployments are secondary/satellite to Bitcoin operations

D6: No factory contracts with enumerable children identified in surface searches; protocol architecture features vault contracts rather than factory-spawned pools; skip factory enumeration for this protocol

D7: Identified contract roles: enzoBTC (token role on Ethereum), lorenzo_vault_contract (vault role, likely admin/governance-controlled), BANK token (governance token on BNB Chain), FBTC_Vault (fund custody contract). Actor classes not yet determined from fetched sources
D8: All enumerated addresses source from fetched URLs (ethplorer.io, github.com search results, DeFiLlama); no snippet-only sightings recorded as admin_addresses

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 2 smart contract addresses (enzoBTC token on Ethereum mainnet and Sepolia testnet) plus references to vault and governance contracts from GitHub and audit materials. Discovery incomplete due to protocol's native Bitcoin focus: Bitcoin layer does not expose traditional smart contract addresses for discovery via block explorers. GitHub repositories contain deployment logic but lack pinned broadcast artifacts. No addresses yet identified for primary admin/governance roles. Classification remains 'unknown' pending access to fuller deployment documentation or on-chain state inspection on BNB Chain (documented as primary EVM chain).

evidence.shows: enzoBTC (Lorenzo Wrapped Bitcoin) token contract on Ethereum; 0.00180326 total supply, 24 transactions, 18 holders as of search date
enzoBTC token contract on Sepolia testnet; 253.34502203 total supply, 14 transactions, 10 holders

Official Lorenzo Protocol documentation homepage; navigation structure includes sections for Governance Token (BANK), Financial Abstraction Layer, and Bitcoin Liquidity Layer; no standalone deployed contracts listing page observed

Lorenzo Protocol GitHub organization; 29 repositories including enzoBTC_contract, lorenzo_vault_contract, lorenzo (main appchain), lorenzo-sdk, audit-report, and infrastructure repos (ethermint fork, DefiLlama adapters)
Solidity smart contract repository for enzoBTC; Foundry tooling; 56 commits on enzoBTC branch; no deployment artifacts visible in browsed README; last updated July 11, 2025

Audit reports repository; audits conducted May 2025 by third-party firms covering vault contracts and staking mechanisms; identified minor reentrancy risks in FBTC_Vault and enzoBTC contracts, subsequently patched
DeFiLlama protocol page; $418.4M TVL (Bitcoin denomination), categorized as Bridge; links to website and GitHub; no specific deployed address list provided in page excerpt

unknowns: D2-deployments: No canonical 'Deployed Contracts' documentation page found; attempted fetch of https://www.lorenzo-protocol.xyz/docs failed (permission denied). GitBook docs do not include explicit address listing per chain.
D4-broadcast: No Foundry broadcast artifacts or Hardhat deployment JSONs discovered in GitHub repositories. Deployment addresses may be managed off-repo or in private deployment systems.

D5-Bitcoin-addresses: Protocol.chains = 'Bitcoin' indicates native Bitcoin operations; Bitcoin does not support traditional smart contracts. No Bitcoin Script addresses or UTXOs discoverable via standard block explorers (Bitcoin blockchain is UTXO-based, not account/address-based like EVM). Likely requires Bitcoin-specific tooling or Lorenzo's own API.

D5-BNB-Chain: Documentation states Lorenzo built 'primarily on BNB Chain' with BANK token (governance) deployed there, but no specific EVM contract addresses (admin, proxy, vault) on BNB Chain discovered in this run. Requires separate on-chain inspection of BNB Chain via explorer or defipunkd API.
D6-factory: No factory contracts identified; confirm whether vault contracts are singleton or factory-generated in next run.

D7-admin-discovery: No explicit admin addresses, multisig wallets, timelock contracts, or governance proxies enumerated. Requires on-chain state inspection (reading owner(), admin(), governance() methods) on deployed EVM contracts.
D8-offchain: Snapshot contains grading_basis='mixed' due to reliance on off-chain GitHub and GitBook documentation for roles and architecture without corresponding on-chain state reads to confirm.

protocol_metadata.about: Lorenzo Protocol is a Bitcoin liquidity finance layer enabling users to stake native BTC and access wrapped token products (stBTC, enzoBTC, YATs) across EVM chains via Wormhole and LayerZero. The protocol tokenizes staked Bitcoin into liquid principal tokens and yield-accruing assets, using a decentralized committee hosting network with BTC-MPC multi-signatures and trusted custodians (Cobo, Ceffu, Chainup) to manage custody. BANK is the native governance and utility token deployed on BNB Chain, enabling protocol governance, revenue sharing, and staking privileges.
